sourceText:
NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for First Quarter Fiscal 2027

May 20, 2026

Record revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% from a year ago
Record Data Center revenue of $75.2 billion, up 92% from a year ago
NVIDIA announces $80.0 billion additional share repurchase authorization and increases its quarterly cash dividend from $0.01 per share to $0.25 per share

SANTA CLARA, Calif., May 20, 2026 — NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) today reported record revenue for the first quarter ended April 26, 2026, of $81.6 billion, up 20% from the previous quarter and up 85% from a year ago.

For the quarter, GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins were 74.9% and 75.0%, respectively.

For the quarter, GAAP and non-GAAP earnings per diluted share were $2.39 and $1.87, respectively.

During the first quarter of fiscal 2027, NVIDIA returned a record level of approximately $20.0 billion to shareholders in the form of shares repurchased and cash dividends. As of the end of the first quarter, the company had $38.5 billion remaining under its share repurchase authorization.

Q1 Fiscal 2027 Summary (GAAP)
Revenue: $81,615M (Q1 FY27) vs $68,127M (Q4 FY26) vs $44,062M (Q1 FY26) — Q/Q +20%, Y/Y +85%
Gross margin: 74.9% (Q1 FY27) vs 75.0% (Q4 FY26) vs 60.5% (Q1 FY26)
Operating expenses: $7,621M (Q1 FY27) vs $6,794M (Q4 FY26) vs $5,030M (Q1 FY26) — Q/Q +12%, Y/Y +52%
Operating income: $53,536M (Q1 FY27) vs $44,299M (Q4 FY26) vs $21,638M (Q1 FY26) — Q/Q +21%, Y/Y +147%
Net income: $58,321M (Q1 FY27) vs $42,960M (Q4 FY26) vs $18,775M (Q1 FY26) — Q/Q +36%, Y/Y +211%
Diluted EPS: $2.39 (Q1 FY27) vs $1.76 (Q4 FY26) vs $0.76 (Q1 FY26) — Q/Q +36%, Y/Y +214%

Data Center compute revenue was a record $60.4 billion, up 77% from a year ago and up 18% sequentially.
Data Center networking revenue was a record $14.8 billion, up 199% from a year ago and up 35% sequentially.

Outlook:
Revenue is expected to be $91.0 billion for Q2 FY27, plus or minus 2%.
GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins are expected to be 74.9% and 75.0%, respectively, plus or minus 50 basis points.
GAAP and non-GAAP operating expenses are expected to be approximately $8.5 billion and $8.3 billion, respectively.

Highlights:
- Announced the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform
- Entered production with NVIDIA Dynamo 1.0
- Expanded collaboration with Google Cloud
- Strategic partnership with Marvell via NVIDIA NVLink Fusion
prompt: Create a compelling 1-minute investor update video summarizing NVIDIA's record-breaking Q1 FY2027 results. Lead with the headline revenue figure, showcase the explosive data center growth, break down revenue segments with charts, highlight the dramatic EPS and net income gains, and close with the bullish Q2 outlook. Keep the tone authoritative and confident — this is a powerhouse quarter.
